summary: Completed backend+frontend verification on preview URL for purchasing/auth/checkout/docs flows. Backend API regression passed (11/11). UI flows for landing, signup, signin, dashboard, developer docs, and Stripe redirect were validated.
action_items: No blocking issues found in requested flows.
Optional hardening: tighten CORS policy (avoid wildcard origins with credentials enabled).
Optional: configure/disable Vercel Analytics script to remove recurring 404 console noise.
critical_code_review_comments: CORS is configured as allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True in backend/server.py; this is insecure and can be rejected by browsers under credentialed requests.
Frontend API env key uses NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL (works), but platform guideline references REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL; unify naming across docs/tooling to prevent future misconfiguration.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ and ui_test_ prefixed customer accounts/orders through API/UI flows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Rate-limit tests can temporarily trigger 429 for subsequent auth/signup checks from same IP. Clear db.rate_limits between aggressive auth-limit tests and UI auth testing if needed.
rca of the issue: Observed temporary UI signup failure (429) after intentionally exercising auth rate-limit endpoint. Root cause: shared IP-based limiter bucket state from prior tests. Mitigation: isolate rate-limit test to end-of-suite or clear rate_limits collection before UI auth tests.
